Hey, welcome back to StraightLine Marketing, Module 4. Now this module is all about funnels. We touched on this in Module 2, but now we're going to delve much, much deeper. You learned in Module 2 that funnels are essentially a way of increasing the value of each and every custom. Now I'm going to take that knowledge to the next level, as right before your eyes, we're going to build powerful and hugely profitable funnels using the principles of StraightLine Marketing. I'm even going to give you a pre-built funnel you can start using in your business today. So this module is imperative to your success as a marketer. So play close attention, take those notes, and again, I'll see you on the other side. The StraightLine customer funnel. In this module, we'll switch gears now and take a much deeper dive into online marketing. We first began assembling this course. We thought about placing the traffic module ahead of this module, but I decided against that, and here's why. You see, website traffic is never an issue. In fact, website traffic has largely been commoditized. What I mean by that is once you're out of buy traffic, you'll never have a traffic problem again. Simply go to the appropriate networks. Make sure you've correctly targeted your audience. You give them the credit card, and that's how simple it is. The real challenge though is determining how much you can spend on traffic. Remember this timeless business rule. The business that's able and willing to spend the most amount of money to acquire a customer will almost always get them. See, in order to outperform your competitors, you need to find a way that enables you to spend more money acquiring customers than they can afford. Now there's only two ways you can do that. One, have higher converting sales files than your competition does, and two, have a higher customer value than your competition does. Sounds confusing, right? Well, don't worry. They're going to go through this in detail with you, and by the end of this, you'll know exactly how to build your own high converting online sales funnel. It radically increases the value of each and every customer you have. So over to you, Dave. Hey guys, David here again, ready to talk you guys through what I consider to be the single most important module, maybe the single most important concept in business altogether, especially in online marketing. And that is of course, the famous sales funnel. Now it's the only way for you guys to literally 10X your business. So whatever you're making now, if you guys are brand new, it's a great way to start building your business. For those of you who have a business in place, it is the only way, in my opinion, for you guys to truly 10X that business and do so almost overnight, provided of course you guys follow the fundamental six steps to the straight line marketing funnel, which we're going to be covering throughout this module. All right, guys. So there's only one rule that matters in business, one rule that stands above all the others. If you know nothing else, but know this simple rule, I'm confident you are going to be way ahead of almost everybody playing in your space or your competitors. In other words, everybody else in your market. And that rule is whoever is able and willing to spend the most amount of money to acquire a customer wins. And I know Jordan has gone through this with you guys very quickly, but I'm wanting to stress it. I want to impress upon you guys just how important it is to be able to spend more money to acquire a customer. Now, though long gone are the days where we would build a business with the mentality of build and they'll come. Build a great business, have a great product, and the universe is fair enough and the markets are fair enough so that the customers will just come flooding through our doors and we'll have to worry about nothing outside of building and delivering a key car product. I wish that was the case. I really do wish that those of you who have the best products in your market would always win, but sadly it's not. Now what we need to do, the only way you guys are going to win and the only way you guys are going to create a defensible business, meaning a sustainable business or a business model where you've got a great business, great product, great business model, and you're confident and convinced that even if a major player, say a leader in a similar space decides to move into your segment, you're still safe. You're still safe. No one's going to take much market share away from you. And the only way you can protect yourself against that onslaught is by of course being able to spend more than that particular business in acquiring a customer. Now remember, business isn't always fair. Everybody's after the same market. I know it's often taught that business isn't a zero sum game and that may be right and it is certainly a romantic way to look at business. But my personal and professional experience, it isn't the worst idea to think of a market as just that, a capped market, a market that has a certain value, a market that has a certain size and a whole bunch of competitors are trying to take as much market share as possible. And if you're mindful of that attitude, you know that you need to beat out those competitors so you can acquire as much market share as possible. So it's mandatory that you know exactly how your competitors are acquiring customers, how your competitors are able to gather and build market share and you need to be able to beat them at the step. Now here is the reality. Your competitors are spending a certain amount of money, whether they know it or not, whether they focus on this metric or not, besides the point, your competitors are spending a certain amount of money to acquire every single customer. Now that money can be broken up in a million different ways, but for our purposes, we're going to look at how much they spend sending traffic to, for instance, a particular sales funnel. A sales funnel of theirs could be something as simple as sending them straight to a sales letter or straight to a sales message. You guys are going to be much smarter than that. You're going to have a much more sophisticated sales funnel that has all these steps and you're able to extract as much value from the customer as possible. But say hypothetically, your customers aren't as smart as you and what they've decided to do is they'll spend $10 on advertising. Let's keep it really simple. In the attempt or expecting to only acquire one customer. Okay, so they'll spend the $10. They'll advertise to 10 people with the $10. Let's keep it really simple. So they spend $10 on Facebook advertising for the objective to market to 10 people knowing that their conversion rate is 10%. So what does that mean? They're spending $10. The message is going out to 10 people. One of those 10 will likely respond and enter the sales funnel, make a purchase, and therefore they have in effect purchased one customer for $10. And that's their metric. They might be selling something for $11. So as far as they're concerned, as long as we can spend $10 and make $11, we've got $1 profit. Right. Now, here's the thing. It is imperative that if they're spending $10, imperative to you guys that if they're spending, and this is the major competitor, the market leader, if the market leader is spending $10 to acquire that one customer, you need to be able to spend $11 or $12. Now what does that mean? That means you're going to be, you're going to be ahead of the curve and you're going to be beating them and moving into leadership at much at a much faster rate than they are because you can of course grow your business at a much faster rate and grow and collect a much greater market share. And you're only able to do that because you are of course able to spend more money in acquiring a customer. Now if you're spending $12 to acquire that same customer, then you're always going to be ahead because you can buy more traffic, right? But you'll compare them. I can't spend $12 because we know they're only making $11 for the sale. Remember, so they're spending 10, they're making 11, so they've got that $1 profit, but you on the other hand are spending 12. So you can grow a lot faster, but if you have the same sales funnel that they had, then it would be impossible for them to be able to spend 12 because, or for you in the case that you're running the same kind of sales funnel as they are, because if you're spending 12 and you're only making back 11, you're in the red $1. You're losing a dollar every time you try to acquire a customer. So how do we solve this problem? How do we equip or enable ourselves to be able to spend more than their competitors while profiting? While continuing to be a coin concern, while continuing to maintain profit and making money? Well, here's the secret. I mean, here's how we do it. The only way that you can spend more money in your competitors in acquiring, excuse me, customers is to increase the value of each customer. That customer needs to be worth more to you than it is to your competitors. Now when I say the only way, it's a bit of a misnomer because there are a few ways to be truthful and honest. I mean, you can increase the conversion rate, for example. I mean, you convert at a higher rate. We took the example, of course, with our competitors converting at 10%. They would spend the $10 and target 10 people and only one person would respond. Of course, if they increase that conversion rate to 20%, as opposed to 10%, then that same $10 would have two people respond. Right? So you can, of course, increase your conversion rate. And there's 100 different ways we can do that. The most powerful way is, of course, copywriting. You guys are familiar with that. We can do split tests, split test, different variables throughout our sales funnel to further increase the conversions. And we'll go through all that. But when I put this course together with Jordan, we were all mindful of the cost of the 80-20 principle. Now, those of you who are unfamiliar with the Pareto principle, it's the idea that 80% of the results we achieve are really, in fact, a product of 20% of the effort. So 80% of results, 20% of efforts. 80% of the consequences are typically related to 20% of the causes. And 80% of our results, 20% of our efforts, 80% of our output is typically related directly to 20% of our input. So we know that there's an imbalance. OK, so not everything is equal. So when I say that it's the only way for you guys to increase, to be able to acquire more customers is to excuse me, the only way for you guys to be able to acquire more customers to increase the value of each customer. It is a little bit of a misnomer, but it really is that 20% that controls 80% of the results. OK, so we know that there are other factors, but this is it. I mean, this is the big thing. If you focus exclusively on increasing the value of every customer, then I don't care how well you'll compare this by traffic. Not all that important. I don't care how aggressive they are with their split testing and multivariate testing and their obsession with conversion rates doesn't really matter because you guys are focusing on that 20% that is responsible for 80% of the results. And if you guys can increase the value of every customer, man, you're way ahead of the competitors, way ahead, because they're not thinking like this, right? They're simply not. They're thinking about their simple core product. They're thinking about sending traffic to that core product and trying to increase conversion slightly by split testing headlines or producing an entirely different sales letter or a video solo or whatever. They're not thinking in terms of building kick ass, bad ass sales funnels. But you guys are. So let's talk about the way we can increase the value of every customer who enters our sales funnel. OK, so a sales funnel is the way to increase the value of every single customer. I wanted to have the same kind of impact on you. Remember, guys, if a customer is worth more to you, then he he or she is worth your competitors. You can spend more money on that customer. Remember, you can spend more money on the customer. If you can spend more money acquiring the customer, then what's stopping you from growing two, three, 10, 20 times? Not on your current rate, but the rate of the market leader. What's stopping you? Absolutely nothing. Nothing is stopping you because you know that for every time you spend $10, you know, you're going to be able to make 20. So what's stopping you from spending a thousand, two thousand, ten thousand dollars a day? Absolutely nothing. You're dropping in quarter at the top of the sales funnel and two quarters are coming out, right? Dropping a dollar at the top and two dollars are coming out. What's stopping you repeating that process over and over and over again? So let's talk about how we go. We're going to construct that kick us about our sales funnel that's going to one hour enable us excuse me to drop a quarter or drop a dollar at the top of the funnel and expect with confidence, two dollars is coming out. Here it is. The six step SLM sales funnel formula. Now this is it, guys. Please pay attention to every single step. There are four mandatory steps, mandatory, many. I don't care what business you're in. You better be doing at least four of these steps. Now the other two are optional. Now here are the all six steps. First, we need an opt-in bribe. You guys are familiar with opt-in bribes. You're familiar with opt-in pages, lead pages, squeeze pages. We know all those terms are interchangeable and we've spoken about what the lead page is, what an opt-in page is, right? So it's a simple page that offers that some kind of a bribe or some kind of an incentive for the visitor to leave their email address and their name or to leave their information in order for them to access that particular bribe. Usually the bribe is, of course, for free, right? We have an opt-in page. The next step in our sales funnel is our self liquidating offer. That's a lot. Now, a self liquidating offer had gotten that name because basically the idea was it's going to offset the money you spend on acquiring traffic or purchasing traffic. So for instance, the objective, of course, is to break even at the SLO level. So if I'm spending $10 on traffic, right, I want my self liquidating offer to give me $10 back. So it's a small little offer with only one objective. And that objective is, of course, just to break even. So if I'm spending $10, I want $10 back and I'm going to use my SLO to get the $10 back. Now, of course, that doesn't mean we're growing, right? Nothing's happened. All we've gotten is an email list as a product of our SLO. We've got an email list. We've sold them something very cheap. That's paid for our traffic. And all we've got is an email list. We haven't grown. We haven't made any money. We haven't made any profit, right? So the profit is going to come at step three of the sales funnel, which is our core product. This is where we make our money. This is our big moneymaker. This is where we experience and enjoy the most amount of margin for this particular product. Right. And we're going to go through examples. So it's all going to be second nature to you guys by the end of this module, I promise. I'm going to do it in a fun way. Fun, exciting, nice and easy. Right now, the fourth and fifth are the optional levels. This, these exist to really extract the most amount of value from each of our customers. All right. So we want our customers to be worth heaps to us, right? We don't want them to be worth what they are worth to our competitors. We want them to be worth much more. And the only way we're going to be able to accomplish that is if we make them more, more of offers, excuse me, at that point of sale. All right. So while they've just purchased and they said, yeah, okay, I'm interested in your self-liquidating offer, let's take a step back. So yeah, I'm interested in that free offer. You've made me, here's my email address. Give me that offer. We've given them the offer. And then you, you've presented them with another offer, but it's really cheap. So this is another thing, you know, I know you're interested, for example, in weight loss because you've taken advantage of this weight loss e-book or this free weight loss guide. You know, for $7, I can give you a more advanced guide or I can give you a guide with you that includes all the missing chapters that might not be included in the opt-in bribe that you just taken advantage of. It's only $7. And because it's an impulse buy, it's so cheap, it's only $7, you're going to get a really high conversion rate, right? So you've successfully crossed them over to your self-liquidating offer. And you've done so successfully. They've converted. Now we haven't made any money, but we spent $7 on them and we've gotten $7 back. So liquidating offer. Now, point three, this is where we make our margin. So for instance, now we're onto a core offer and now it's not as simple as a $7 product. But now we might be selling them a couple of hundred dollars, right? A couple of hundred dollars, some piece of exercise equipment, for example, and we're going to make a significant margin from that. Now they've just purchased that core product. Now we're in the green or now we're in the black. We've actually made some profit. We're not going to just let them go. Okay, we can let them go there and a lot of businesses do let them go there. But we have an optional fourth and fifth step, but we can make them another upsell at that point of sale. So you purchase the exercise equipment. Now all the smithing is you want, you know, you get nutritional elements. So we're going to sell you some supplements, take advantage of these supplements. Since you've already bought these core products from us and you purchased the e-book from us and you've taken advantage of the free weight loss guide, you know, what's really going to complete the set is, you know, it's a kilogram of protein, whatever. And since you've gone through all those steps with us, we'll give you that protein at a heavily discounted rate. So now that cost customer or client worth even more to you because they've gotten the e-book, they've gotten the, the lost chapters, they've gotten the exercise equipment. Now they've gotten also the supplements, the protein. Okay. So now they're worth a whole lot more than you than a simple information product company that's only selling e-books. Or they're worth a whole lot more than you than a company, for example, it sells exercise equipment, it's exclusive, right? Because they, they put a lot more from you than a simple piece of exercise equipment. Now here's the next optional step. Say hypothetically, they say, no, say, okay, I'm not interested in the supplement. Now, you know, I think the price of the supplements are a little too expensive. You can offer them a downsell. A downsell could be something as simple as, you know, stripping some of the features of that up. So, okay, so we're going to sell you that one kilogram of protein. How about you only buy five, five, five grams of, five hundred, excuse me, grams of protein instead. And I'll prepare to offer it to you at a particular rate or a competitive rate. It's only 500 grams. You know, it's not going to give you the kind of impact that the four one kilogram of protein will do, but it will at least give you an opportunity to try this protein and see the impact that balancing your nutrition together with applying the principles in the ebook and taking advantage of the exercise equipment will really have on turning around your life and helping you achieve your goals. So there's a downsell there, right? So they said, no, the upsell, but we've come back and we've offered them something else. Why? Because we're going to extract the most amount of value from that particular prospect or from that client or customer who's entered our sales funnel. So those are the two optional steps. Now, back to the next mandatory step, which is step six. And that's our funnel follow up. Now, that's all about guys who have dropped off somewhere in our funnel. For instance, they've purchased our or they've taken advantage of our opt-in bribe, but then they said no to our self-liquidating offer. OK, so we want them. All right, we're not prepared to give up on you just yet, right? Because we paid money to send traffic to that opt-in page. We're not prepared to just let you guys go. We need you guys to come back and purchase that. So, hello, that self-liquidating offer or guys will purchase the self-liquidating offer and then said no to our core product. I mean, we've spent money on acquiring you guys. We spent money on sending traffic to the opt-in page and we've converted it so well and got you guys to take advantage of our rest of the low. We're not prepared to let you guys go and just disappear after having not purchased our real moneymaker, our core product. So what do we do? We put together a follow up system or a system that encourages people to move to the next step of the sales funnel that they hadn't or we hadn't been able to successfully convert them to. So if we drop off from one to two, we put in a sales funnel, sorry, a funnel follow up that encourages them to move and convert to step three. So move on to step two successfully and move on to step three. If we've lost them at step three, so they haven't purchased the core offer, but they have taken advantage of the SLO. We have a funnel follow up system that encourages them to, of course, make purchase of the core product and then continue to move down the sales funnel and thereby extracting the most amount of value from every customer who enters our sales funnel. So of course, the ultimate objective here again, and I'm going to repeat this for the entire module, whether or not you're sick of it, sick of hearing it. It is the most important concept in business. I'm going to keep on repeating it. The reason we're doing all of this is, of course, so that we can extract the most amount of value from our customers or from every prospect that enters our lease or enters our sales funnel, thereby allowing us to spend more than our competitors in acquiring that same prospect or customer. OK, let's take a look at this in live action. All right, good friend of JordanBellfoot.com, Anthony Robbins. Now, what does Anthony do for a living? I mean, how does Anthony make his money? We know that Tony Robbins makes almost all of his money through two different ways. One is through selling high end information products that are priced to $4,000 or $5,000. Now, we know Tony making money from selling high end information products and sending people to or filling up auditoriums for his seminars. But look what he's done here. How has he got people into the sales funnel? Has he made an offer on Facebook or if he made an offer on Google, encouraging people to attend a seminar, people who have no idea who he is, maybe, or people who have never attended a seminar before? Of course not. He's taking them through step one of the six step sales funnel. And what's step one? It's a free opt-in bribe. So let's take a look at Tony's. We've had a credibility element up the top over two million copies sold. And then we have a compelling headline. And of course, they can take advantage of that opt-in bribe, which is the free ebook. Right. So we have to do is enter your name and your email address and we'll send you the download link right away. I think this is a little asking for a little too much only because we've said the first name is optional. Right. And it has its advantages and that we can personalize our messages when we put them into our order responder. And those of you who are familiar with those terms, sit tight, hang loose. It's going to be really easy and we're going to go through all that soon. But for now, we said that you may or may not include the first name, right? But we never even mentioned last name, unless, of course, we're talking about the lead gen page, where we need to extract so much information to give to a salesperson. But in this particular case, since the information isn't going to a salesperson, and we know it isn't going to a salesperson because they've reason to number their or a physical address, the expecting the prospects to include their last name, too, I'd advise against it only because I've run so many tests and including the last name, too, seems to significantly and dramatically dip conversions. I mean, they really do reduce our conversion rate. And it's not really important. I mean, why do we need that prospect surname? You may think of yourself as a prospect who has just seen this advertisement and put yourself in the prospect shoes and think, would you be comfortable putting in your surname? You may say yes, or you may say, oh, I'll do it. But, you know, I don't know why I'm doing this or I'll put a fake surname. But you can see the idea that there was some conflict in your mind when you thought about that last name. Your prospects are going to be thinking the same way. It's not really important. So you can reduce that. You can strip the opt-in page of that particular element and you can just have first name and email address. Alternatively, you can go even one step further and really improve your conversion rate by just having the email address and not having the first name at all. But the purpose of this particular slide is to impress upon you guys the importance of an opt-in page. It's the first step of a sales funnel. Everybody who knows what they're doing is doing this. At least anyone who's accomplishing any real success is doing an opt-in page. They're trying to gather as much data as possible. They don't sell their core product right away. Tony is not selling his seminar right away. What he's doing here is encouraging people to get into a sales funnel by, of course, submitting their information to, of course, take advantage of a free ebook. That's our opt-in type. Let's look at step two, our self-liquidating offer. Now, I thought about what kind of SLO can I really present you guys that you're going to be familiar with and it's going to get your heads turning and you're going to think, oh, that's why they do it. I've seen that before. And the best one I could come up with is the $2 menu. Right. So the $2 menu is popular in throughout almost every fast food chain, at least the ones that are accomplishing any major success right now. I know in Australia, the $2 menu is very popular in both KFC and McDonald's in the states. But imagine it's the same. What's the purpose of the $2 offer? The $2 menu is McDonald's really making any money from a 30 cent soft serve ice cream cone. The answer simply is no. Do you think McDonald's is making any money at all from a $1 soft drink? Of course not. $1 fries, of course not. They couldn't even make any business. They couldn't be a going concern if they were selling $1 fries to everybody who's coming through the drive-thru. They purchased a $1 fries or a 30 cent ice cream cone and driven out the other end. It's impossible. Right. But they're using these self-liquidating offers just so they can get people into the store and then move them down their sales funnel. OK. That's exactly what's happening. This is simply a self-liquidating offer. The objective here is just to get people into the sales funnel, part with their money because anybody who's purchased anything from you is 10 times more likely to purchase something. else from you than somebody who's never bought anything at all. Right. So let's get these guys to become customers. Let's turn this prospect into a customer. And the simplest way to do that is sell them something that's dirt cheap. McDonald's is doing it with $2 offers, $2 menu items, 30 cent menu items, $1 menu items. KFC is doing the same thing. Invariably, McDonald's comes up with a good idea. Good idea. Excuse me, Burger King KFC. I'm going to jump on it pretty quickly. I mean, historically, that's what happens. But that's exactly what happened in this particular case. We've got a simple little offer that's encouraging people to just step into McDonald's, make a small purchase, become a customer, and then McDonald's. It's now the job of their sales funnel to move you guys down to step three, step four, step five, step six. Right. So let's look at what McDonald's will do after, for example, you take an advantage of a 30 cent ice cream cone or a $1 soft drink. Step three, core product. See, you just purchased a $1 small Coke or a 30 cent ice cream cone. Typically, their sales funnel will move you down to a core product. Now, McDonald's does this through what we call push marketing as opposed to pull pursuit marketing. So they don't actually pursue you guys and say, all right, so now it's time to buy your core product. Now it's time to take advantage of our core offer, our Big Mac. Rather, they'll just put very attractive and exciting imagery like this picture of this Big Mac, for example. You've just purchased the ice cream cone or you've just purchased the $1 drink. And now you can see all these great, great, good looking burgers standing in front of you and it becomes almost irresistible. And you say, OK, I'm going to take advantage of that core offer. You're not saying it in those terms, of course, you're just saying, well, I'm here and I've just purchased the drink for a dollar. Might as well get a Big Mac with that. All right. So now they're actually making some money. They weren't at first, right? They're not going to make any money from the 30 cent ice cream cone. They're not going to make any money from that $1 Coke. But at the core product level, they're actually starting to make some money. They're starting to make a respectable margin from whatever it is that they're offering you. Right. So now you've purchased a Coke and you've purchased a and you purchased a Big Mac with some significant margin. Excuse me, attached to it. Are they going to let you go? Is Hasmok Donald successfully extracted the most amount of money from you that they possibly could? Or do you think that they can offer you something else? I bet you're all saying, no, they're probably going to offer us something else. And I'm sure you all have a good idea of what the next upsell is going to be. So what's that for the sales funnel? McDonald's sales funnel. It's your upsell offer. We like fries with that. They're not prepared to let you go just yet. You've purchased a small Coke. You purchased a their core product, which they're Big Mac, and now they want to offer you an upsell. In this case, they're going to ask you on fries with that. What do you want to turn that into a meal? No, you've got your $1 small Coke and you've got your your Big Mac. Now you can actually take advantage of the chips as well. Take advantage of our upsell offer. And we can put in a meal for you and reduce the price. So what is McDonald's effectively done there? They've gotten you to purchase their SLO. They've gotten you to purchase their core offer. And now they've gotten you to purchase an upsell offer. Now they're making the big bucks. If people are doing this, everybody's going to McDonald's and buying a Big Mac meal. The margins are awesome. They can continue to do this all day. They can spend millions and millions of dollars on national or global advertising through television and radio platforms, direct mail or online. And they can do it in confidence because they know that for every five dollars they spend in acquiring one McDonald's customer. And that customer is going to spend seven or eight dollars on a Big Mac meal. And that's exactly how it's done. All right, so let's move away from McDonald's example as we go through step five and six. Downsell offers. Now, this was a particular downsell that I wanted to include for you guys, only because it was unique and different and lets you know exactly how much creativity is at play when producing your sales funnel. In this particular case, it was for an organization or an online business that sells stock images and for those of you unfamiliar, if you want to, for instance, purchase images as opposed to having to take them yourself. And you can put them in your marketing material or you can put that particular image online on your websites or on your sales funnels, whatever. You typically have to buy the picture, right? So you have a picture or a very, really attractive girl that converts really well, for example, and you have an ebook, a company ebook with it that sells a weight loss product. It's not it's not required for you to actually go and try to find the pretty girl and take a photo of her rather. You can of course, just purchase that photo online through a photo stock company. And this is one such company. What's their core product here? Here, their core product is a pro subscription plan. They give you lots of options, 12 months, six months, three months, one month. The idea is they their core product is a continuity product, a product that requires you to pay every single month. So they want you to sign up for a 12 month plan. That's 649 right on the outset. If you're not prepared to sign up for a one month, that you can sign up for a six or three month plan. That's fine. You can even sign up for a one month plan. The reason they really want you guys to sign up for the plan is because you're going to be billed every month. And that means you're going to be worth a whole lot more to them than you would be. If you had just purchased one off photograph, right? So they want you guys to enter into their continuity program, but you don't want to. You're not prepared to sign up for 12 months, not prepared to sign up for six months, not prepared to sign up for one month. But they spent a lot of money in showing you this advertisement. They spent a lot of money in getting you this far down their sales funnel. All right, they've had some really fantastic copy. They've driven traffic to that particular page through a Facebook or whatever traffic channel they've used. And you've got all the way down to the pricing plan. They spent a lot of money. They're not prepared to let you go. All right, they want to extract as much value from you as possible. So what have they done? You can see they're in the highlighted rectangle box. They've created a pay as you go option. Now, these pay as you go option is not their plan. Hey, they're not excited about this. They're not excited about you buying 50 credits, which may convert to 10 or 12 photos online or 10 or 12 stock photographs and then disappearing and never coming back again. That's not their plan. But since you said no to everything else, they're going to offer you a down. So in this particular case, the downsell is, all right, let's just convert this prospect into a customer, at least they've given us some money. Now that you've given us some money and they can take advantage of our great service, we can put them back into our funnel at some point and then slowly but surely work at them until they do actually purchase one of our core offers, which of course is one of the continuity, pro subscription plans. So let's a downsell. So a downsell doesn't need to be simply a reduction in price. All right, so you'd OK. You didn't want to purchase that at this price. What do you want to purchase at this heavily discounted price? It doesn't need to be something so obnoxious. Frankly, I don't like that. It could be something a little bit more creative and artistic, something like a pay-as-you-go as opposed to a continuity. Or it could be your core product with a few features stripped, right? So you don't need to offer them, for example, if you're selling an information product with a whole series of bonuses, rather than offering a downsell, we just reduce the price of the entire pack. I think that's a bad move, frankly. I think it's unfair for the people who have purchased the full asking price. You can say, OK, I'll give you guys the actual core information product, but I'll strip it of the bonuses. And now that the bonuses are gone, I can present this offer to you guys at a discounted rate and that can function as your downsell. So the objective here is if they say no, don't let them go offer them either something else or a payment plan or a different or a different product, entirely a cheaper product or the same product with a few features stripped. All right, so that's our downsell offer. Now, let's look at our sixth and final step of our six step SLM sales funnel. And that is our funnel following. So for those of you who are, and I expect everybody to be familiar with the three 10s now, if you're not, please stop here and go back to some of the fundamental modules and make sure you're familiar with it. But the three 10s is we need to sell ourselves, we need to sell our product, and of course we need to sell our company. And if any of those three 10s aren't satisfied, it's typically why that visitor decided to bounce and not move on to the next step of the sales funnel. So it's the job of our retargeting in this particular case to sell one or more of the three 10s that we failed to successfully sell at the earlier stage of our sales funnel. And that's what we're going to be using. We're going to be using those three 10s as we move across our retargeting module, and we're going to talk about exactly how to get somebody who hasn't converted from step two to three, for example, to actually do so using retargeting by selling or advertising or promoting, of course, our three 10s. And this sales page is selling the SLO, our self liquidating offer, our cheap offer. The objective here is just to break even, right? We're not trying to make any money by selling them this cheap offer. We're just trying to pay for the traffic that we've just spent or get back what it already is we spent on traffic. So we have a SLO and that's going to go to the order forms. They've clicked, yes, click now, sorry, purchase here, add to cart. They've added this and yes, I want your $7 ebook. So they've actually, we have their email address, of course, in the lead capture page. We have their credit card information after having purchased on the order form. And now we're going to actually try to start making some money. All right, then we're going to have a one click upsell. So we have their credit card information on file. We don't need them to input it again. We're just going to present a one click upsell that encourages them to take advantage of an upsell, which is of course our core product. You actually traditionally sell and traditionally make money on, traditionally enjoy a respectable margin on. So that's your higher margin product, your Big Mac. So here they're going to take advantage of our Big Mac. Now we've got our optional options. Excuse me, we're already profiting here, so we're good, but we want to make as much money as possible. We want to extract as much value from every customer as possible. So of course we can spend more than our competitors. So what we're going to do is we're going to offer them the upsell option, upsell option, excuse me, which is of course optional. So they've purchased that core product. Now we're going to try to make even more money from the person who's just visited outside and made this fire in our sales funnel by offering them another upsell. Okay. They've taken advantage of that upsell. They moved to a thank you page. We've extracted as much value as we can from them, but it's not over for them just yet because we have their email address and we may introduce another product or we may have another product already in our products, products as sweet that we want to present or make an offer to them, present or offer to them. So we're going to send them emails about our new products, right? Because we have their email address. We've accomplished having step one of our sales funnel. We knew how important email addresses were. We have that and even though they've made it all the way down down our sales funnel, we've extracted so much value from them and we're already likely to be able to spend much more than our competitors in acquiring this particular customer because this customer is worth a lot more to us than it would be to our competitors. We still have their email address. So we're still going to use that to generate sales and present the prospect or in this case the customer with more offers. Now of course you still have your downsell option. So what about if they take advantage of your upsell but they don't take advantage of your upsell set too? So what are you going to do? You say, okay, you might not want this particular product. You've already purchased, of course, at this point they've given you their email address, take advantage of your opt-in bride. They purchase your SLO. They purchase your core product. They purchase your first upsell and they don't want to buy your second upsell. You can offer them a downsell. Okay, you've purchased all of that. So I know you're serious about wanting to accomplish whatever it is that your product accomplishes or whatever advantages or benefits your product passes on to the consumer. So we know you want to accomplish this particular benefit and I understand that this particular product, which is your second upsell in this case, might not be a perfect fit or whatever it is that's your most common objection. It could be too expensive. So how about you take advantage of this payment plan and then you offer them a downsell. And the reason we offer them that downsell, of course, is we're trying to extract as much as we can from that particular customer. And this is all at point of sale. So we haven't done anything else. You say, this is one page, they've moved on to the next page, they moved on to the next page, the next page. The reason, of course, I put optional in brackets next to the upsells and the downsells is you may be in a market where it's not congruent with your business or not congruent with the way the market functions or operates. And it may make a lot more sense for you to just offer your opt-in page, your core product, your upsell and then move them straight to a thank you page. And that's it. Okay. In some cases, it might be a little slimy and a little too salesy to offer upsell and upsell and then downsell and downsell and upsell. You know, you can get a little convoluted and it may tick off some of your visitors. Some markets not only is it okay, but it's actually expected. And I really encourage you guys to do a whole series of upsells and downsells in other markets. Of course, it might not be your best option. That's obviously something you should use your intuition to decide and also test aggressively. Fine. Man, build a funnel with something like this and then build a funnel with by something like this, something with the whole series, upsells and downsells and then build a funnel that runs straight from upsell to a thank you page. It's one upsell for the thank you page or core offer straight to a thank you page and take a little bit of conversion rates to take a little bit of the amount of value you're able to extract from each customer out both in the short term and long term and make up your mind as to whether or not you want your sales funnel to be so complex and sophisticated or a more simple, crude, raw type sales funnel is more appropriate for your market and of course your offering, your business. All right, guys. All right, guys. So the next question that I'm sure you're all asking is, all right, I get the idea of a six step straight line marketing sales funnel. I understand its value. I know that it's going to increase the value of every single customer I have, which means I can spend more to acquire every customer, which means that I am virtually unstoppable and unbeatable in my business efforts and my marketing efforts. All right, I get it. I believe it. I understand it. But how do I build it? How do I build it? I'm not a web developer. I don't understand, excuse me, how to get these pages up online and I believe in the theory and the strategy of outline for me, but I'm just not sure how to go about actually executing it. Now, that's what we're going to be discussing here. Just how easy it is to actually practically build out a sales funnel, get it up online, send traffic to it and extract as much value from every customer that comes in to your sales funnel as possible. All right, so now we're going to talk about how we're going to go about actually building it. Now, here's the thing, guys. Building your sales funnel is not nearly as difficult as it sounds. Matter of fact, you've actually already done the difficult part. The real challenge to building to sales funnels in general, excuse me, is actually understanding the theory, thinking about why am I doing exactly what I'm doing? Why am I building this particular page? How is it going to benefit me? How am I going to be able to use it to capture as much market share as possible and try to move into leadership and build a business that's defensible against competitors and market leaders who try to come into your segment? That's the challenging part. You guys understand opt-in pages. You understand self-liquidating offers, low-ticket offer items to turn prospects into customers. You understand the value of having a core offer and an immediate upsell and a downsell. You understand return paths and how you're going to get people back into your funnel where they've since dropped out. That's the challenging part. That's the part you guys already understand. If you have any difficulty with that, I really do encourage you guys to go back to the start of the video so you can really develop a strong understanding of the theory. With respect to the practical application, actually building this thing out is actually pretty simple. There are a few tools that will almost do it for you as long as you just apply a lot of the theory that you guys already know. I was actually in two minds. I wasn't sure whether or not I should actually build our sales funnel in front of you guys. If I did, which actual tool I would use because they're all great. There you have WordPress, ClickFunnels, Unbounce, Lead Pages. The only one I'm actually a little less confident about is WordPress only because WordPress is a powerful CMS system and you can absolutely build a KeyGuy sales funnel on WordPress. But it does require a few too many integrations and you have to play around with it a little bit. If you really want to go down that path and you want to build a funnel out of WordPress, I would actually encourage you to just hire a developer, a cheap developer. Go on to Fiverr or go on to Upwork and just hire a developer for a few dollars an hour and just tell them exactly what you want. Use the process map that you guys have in your download area, the funnel map. Use the sample funnel that's also in your members area. For those of you who don't know, we said we were going to give you a funnel. We're doing just that. But of course you guys already, excuse me, understand the power of copywriting and you understand our straight line system for writing copy. I do want you guys to not be limited by the actual template that you have in your access or in your resource area and just use the templates rather as a guide and not as a constraint. Feel free to exercise some creativity with the kind of things you have on your landing pages. Follow the template. It's all there for you.
